root_cause_review.issue: Previous --resume-existing logic reused any existing segment mp4 without checking whether edit_plan/captions/audio/style inputs changed.

Initial fingerprint was too narrow and did not include all render dependencies such as source media, script/style inputs, logo/font assets, profile reports, people map, semantic marks, and sync offsets.
Cache manifests were adjacent sidecars, which made cache state harder to inspect and clean.
Existing segment cache had no manifests and was therefore unsafe to reuse.

implementation_changes: Each reusable segment must have a matching fingerprint manifest in output/videos/preview_test_project1_style_segments/_manifests/.
Fingerprint payload includes the event JSON, render script signature, source media signatures, logo/font assets, people/profile/semantic report signatures, sync offsets, and core style constants.
Invalid or missing cache entries are deleted before re-rendering so stale mp4 files cannot be concatenated after a failed or partial reuse decision.
audit_render_segment_cache.py reports reusable, invalid, missing, orphan segment, and orphan manifest counts before rendering.
current_state: Cache is clean. One smoke-tested segment has a current manifest; the remaining segments are absent and will be rendered fresh next time.
render_performed: Only a one-segment smoke render was performed and its temporary output files were removed. No full preview render was produced.
